samples.xml_description:
<module id="alu">
    <description>ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU. Supports various arithmetic and logical operations including ADD, SUB, AND, OR, XOR, NOR, SLT, SLTU, SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV, and LUI. Outputs results along with flags for zero, carry, negative, overflow, and a general flag for SLT/SLTU operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit input operand B.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">6-bit control signal for selecting the operation to be performed.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">32-bit output representing the result of the operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">1-bit output indicating whether the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">1-bit output indicating whether a carry occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">1-bit output indicating whether the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">1-bit output indicating whether an overflow occurred during the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">1-bit output representing a general flag, which is set based on specific operations (SLT and SLTU).</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="ADD" value="6'b100000" description="Opcode for ADD operation."/>
        <constant name="ADDU" value="6'b100001" description="Opcode for ADDU operation."/>
        <constant name="SUB" value="6'b100010" description="Opcode for SUB operation."/>
        <constant name="SUBU" value="6'b100011" description="Opcode for SUBU operation."/>
        <constant name="AND" value="6'b100100" description="Opcode for AND operation."/>
        <constant name="OR" value="6'b100101" description="Opcode for OR operation."/>
        <constant name="XOR" value="6'b100110" description="Opcode for XOR operation."/>
        <constant name="NOR" value="6'b100111" description="Opcode for NOR operation."/>
        <constant name="SLT" value="6'b101010" description="Opcode for SLT operation."/>
        <constant name="SLTU" value="6'b101011" description="Opcode for SLTU operation."/>
        <constant name="SLL" value="6'b000000" description="Opcode for SLL operation."/>
        <constant name="SRL" value="6'b000010" description="Opcode for SRL operation."/>
        <constant name="SRA" value="6'b000011" description="Opcode for SRA operation."/>
        <constant name="SLLV" value="6'b000100" description="Opcode for SLLV operation."/>
        <constant name="SRLV" value="6'b000110" description="Opcode for SRLV operation."/>
        <constant name="SRAV" value="6'b000111" description="Opcode for SRAV operation."/>
        <constant name="LUI" value="6'b001111" description="Opcode for LUI operation."/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign r[31:0] = res[31:0];>
            Assigns the lower 32 bits of the register (res) to the output (r).
        </assign>
        <assign flag = (aluc == SLT || aluc == SLTU) ? 1 : z;>
            Sets the flag output to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise.
        </assign>
        <assign zero = ~|r ? 1 : 0;>
            Sets the zero output to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Uses parameters to define the control signals for various operations, such as ADD, SUB, AND, OR, etc. 
        The module assigns the input operands to the signed wires and the output result (r) to the lower 32 bits of the register (res[31:0]). The flag output is determined based on the control signal (aluc) and is set to '1' when the operation is SLT or SLTU, and 'z' (high-impedance) otherwise. The zero output is set to '1' when the result is all zeros, and '0' otherwise.
        Inside the always block, a case statement is used to perform the appropriate operation based on the control signal (aluc). The result is assigned to the register (res) accordingly. For shift operations (SLL, SRL, SRA, SLLV, SRLV, SRAV), the shift amount is determined by the value of 'a' or 'a[4:0]'. For the LUI operation, the upper 16 bits of 'a' are concatenated with 16 zeros to form the result.
        If the control signal (aluc) does not match any defined operation, the result is assigned as 'z' (high-impedance).
    </implementation>
</module>
